title: When to Avoid Popular Frameworks
concept: The tradeoffs of heavy agent libraries: ease of onboarding versus lack of control and debugging complexity.

exercises.prompt: What is a common benefit of using popular frameworks?

options.text: They are usually free to use.
They make onboarding easier for new developers.

exercises.explain: The correct answer shows that popular frameworks simplify the process for beginners, making it easier to start working quickly.

cards.title: When to Avoid Popular Frameworks
cards.body: Sometimes, using widely popular frameworks isn't the best choice. They can make things easy at first, but they also have hidden downsides.

cards.title: Heavy Agent Libraries
cards.body: These are popular frameworks designed to help create AI agents. They can save time but may limit control and make debugging harder.

cards.title: The Tool Analogy
cards.body: Using a heavy framework is like using an all-in-one tool that’s easy to use but tricky to fix if it breaks. You might prefer specialized tools that give you more precise control.

cards.title: Example Scenario
cards.body: A beginner developer may quickly get started with a popular library like TensorFlow, but later they find it hard to track down bugs in the complex code it generates.

cards.title: Myth: Frameworks Solve All Problems
cards.body: Many believe that using a framework guarantees success. However, they can limit your understanding of how things work under the hood.

cards.title: Tradeoffs to Consider
cards.body: When choosing a framework, weigh ease of use against control and debugging complexity.

right.text: Limited ability to fix errors and customize.

exercises.prompt: What is a downside of using heavy agent libraries?
exercises.explain: The correct answer highlights that while frameworks make it easier to start, they often add layers of complexity that complicate debugging.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using a popular framework always gives you complete control over your code.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is false. Popular frameworks often abstract away details, limiting control.

exercises.prompt: Heavy agent libraries can save time, but they may limit your ability to __________ certain issues.
exercises.sentence: Heavy agent libraries can save time, but they may limit your ability to debug certain issues.
exercises.explain: Debug is the correct term as it refers specifically to fixing errors in the code.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about heavy agent libraries is written by an AI?

options.text: They can make your project finish faster.
Using them guarantees the best performance.
Choosing them often leads to complex debugging issues.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated option accurately reflects the hidden tradeoff of using popular frameworks.

exercises.prompt: When might it be better to create a custom framework instead of using a popular one?

options.text: When you have very specific project needs.
When you want to use more less powerful tools.
When you need a project done fast and easy.
When everyone else is using it.

exercises.explain: The best answer is that custom solutions are ideal when specific needs must be met that popular frameworks cannot address.
